macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Crypto markets tumble as $86 billion is wiped out in broad selloff
items.summary: Cryptocurrency markets dropped sharply over the past 24 hours, with Bitcoin briefly falling below $75,000 and major altcoins sliding 5% to 9%. The selloff was linked to SEC delays, geopolitical tensions, ETF outflows, and a wave of liquidations affecting nearly 160,000 traders.

items.title: Bitcoin trades near $74,688 after intraday move between $74,290 and $77,585
items.summary: Binance’s market update showed Bitcoin weakening by 3.34% over the prior 24 hours, with the token trading just under $75,000. The update reflects continued pressure across the crypto market as BTC remains highly volatile.

items.title: Bitcoin falls below $77,000, triggering broader crypto market weakness
items.summary: A market update reported Bitcoin slipping below the key $75,000 level, contributing to a wider drop across major cryptocurrencies including Ethereum and other top altcoins. The move also coincided with aggressive trading activity, including a large Bitcoin short position opened by a pseudonymous trader.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Google expands Gemini across Search and AI Mode
items.summary: Google has integrated multiple Gemini models and agentic capabilities more deeply into Search and AI Mode, blending generative assistance with traditional search. The rollout reportedly includes Gemini 3 in AI Mode and Gemini 3.5 Flash as the default for AI Mode and the Gemini app.
items.source: Google Blog / cited in Let’s Data Science News

items.title: Google launches Gemini Omni multimodal family
items.summary: Google announced Gemini Omni, a new multimodal model family, with Gemini Omni Flash as the first released model. The model can reportedly generate and edit video from combined image, audio, video, and text inputs and is being rolled out across the Gemini app, Google Flow, and YouTube Shorts.
items.source: Google Blog / cited in Let’s Data Science News

items.title: Google releases Gemini Spark cloud AI agent
items.summary: Google has introduced Gemini Spark, a cloud-based AI agent aimed at more proactive and task-oriented assistance. The launch fits a broader shift toward agentic AI products that can act on behalf of users rather than only respond to prompts.

items.title: Anthropic acquires developer-tools startup Stainless
items.summary: Anthropic has acquired Stainless, a startup known for automating SDK and MCP server generation for AI labs. The deal was reported to be worth more than $300 million, signaling continued consolidation in the AI developer tooling market.
items.source: The Information / cited in TechCrunch via Let’s Data Science News

items.title: Japan expected to gain access to Anthropic's latest model
items.summary: Japan’s finance minister said the government is expected to gain access to Anthropic’s latest model, Claude Mythos, later this month. The access would also extend to Japanese financial institutions, according to the reported remarks.
items.source: The Straits Times / cited in Let’s Data Science News

items.title: Trump cancels planned AI executive order
items.summary: President Donald Trump reportedly canceled a planned executive order that would have required federal review of new AI models before public release. The move could ease regulatory pressure on model developers in the near term.
items.source: NewsOne / cited in Let’s Data Science News

items.title: Bittensor subnet publishes decentralized pretraining architecture
items.summary: Bittensor’s Subnet 9 has published the Incentivised Orchestrated Training Architecture (IOTA), a decentralized system for splitting model layers across heterogeneous nodes. The design is intended to support permissionless pretraining at scale.
items.source: arXiv / project documentation cited in Let’s Data Science News

items.title: Industry trend: AI products shift toward proactive agents and continuous learning
items.summary: Recent reporting highlights a broader industry move toward proactive AI systems that anticipate user needs, along with reinforcement learning infrastructure aimed at systems that learn continuously from experience. This trend is showing up across consumer products, enterprise workflows, and model infrastructure investments.
items.source: AI Daily / Let’s Data Science News

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Xbox Game Pass confirms June 2025 Wave 1 lineup
items.summary: Microsoft revealed the first wave of games joining Xbox Game Pass in June 2025 ahead of the Xbox Games Showcase. The lineup highlights the latest additions coming to the subscription service.

items.title: Helldivers 2 teases new Force of Law Warbond
items.summary: Arrowhead's cooperative shooter is adding a new Warbond, giving players more gear and cosmetics to unlock. The announcement came as a surprise to fans following the game's ongoing live-service updates.

items.title: Square Enix unveils Final Fantasy Tactics: The Ivalice Chronicles Collector's Box
items.summary: Square Enix revealed a new collector's bundle for Final Fantasy Tactics: The Ivalice Chronicles. The company noted that the box set does not include a copy of the game.

items.title: Former Call of Duty and XDefiant producer says he is stepping away from gaming
items.summary: An executive producer tied to XDefiant announced plans to leave the gaming industry after the game's shutdown. The move adds to a turbulent period for developers affected by studio and live-service closures.

items.title: PlayStation State of Play showcases new controller with unexpected design
items.summary: Sony revealed a new controller during its latest State of Play presentation. The design was one of several hardware and software announcements made during the showcase.

items.title: Nioh 3 announced during PlayStation State of Play
items.summary: Team Ninja and Koei Tecmo officially unveiled Nioh 3 at Sony's June State of Play. The reveal indicates the soulslike series is continuing with a new entry.

items.title: Arc System Works and Sony reveal new Marvel fighting game
items.summary: A brand-new tag-team fighting game featuring Marvel characters was announced during Sony's showcase. The collaboration marks another major crossover for the fighting game genre.

items.title: Bungie layoffs reportedly incoming as Destiny 2 faces pressure
items.summary: GameSpot is reporting claims that significant layoffs may be coming to Bungie. The report comes amid broader scrutiny of the studio as Destiny 2 continues to face challenges.

items.title: Report says GTA 6 is still on track for November launch
items.summary: GameSpot highlights a report stating that Grand Theft Auto 6 has not been delayed again and remains scheduled for a November release. The update will reassure fans watching for any change to the blockbuster's timeline.

items.title: Subnautica 2 early access surpasses 4 million copies in days
items.summary: Recent gaming coverage says Subnautica 2 has crossed 4 million copies sold very quickly after launch. That performance points to strong early demand for the survival sequel.
items.source: Gaming news coverage via YouTube discussion citing Gamatsu

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Apple argues Epic ruling shouldn’t set App Store rules for all developers
items.summary: Apple is pushing back on efforts to broaden the impact of its Epic Games lawsuit, saying the case should not be used to rewrite App Store rules for every developer. The dispute remains one of the most closely watched technology policy battles in the industry.

items.title: FBI warns of Kali365 phishing-as-a-service targeting Microsoft 365 users
items.summary: The FBI has issued a warning about Kali365, a fast-growing phishing kit being used in attacks against Microsoft 365 accounts. Security researchers say the service is helping lower the barrier for credential theft and business email compromise.

items.title: Drupal core SQL injection bug added to CISA’s known exploited vulnerabilities list
items.summary: A SQL injection vulnerability in Drupal core is being actively exploited, prompting inclusion in CISA’s Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. Site operators are being urged to patch immediately to reduce the risk of compromise.

items.title: Microsoft admits Teams interface is too crowded and is working on a fix
items.summary: Microsoft acknowledged criticism that Microsoft Teams’ user interface has become cluttered and said it is developing changes to improve the experience. The update signals a broader effort to simplify one of its most important enterprise products.

items.title: Texas Attorney General sues Meta over WhatsApp encryption claims
items.summary: Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton filed a lawsuit accusing Meta of misleading users about WhatsApp’s end-to-end encryption. The case adds to the growing regulatory scrutiny facing large platform companies over privacy and security claims.

items.title: Apple and hardware update chatter intensifies ahead of new software cycle
items.summary: Industry coverage highlights expectations for an upcoming Apple software update and possible Siri-related changes, alongside broader hardware and service adjustments. While details remain limited, the reports point to another busy product cycle for Apple.
